page: Google Cloud Platform c4-highcpu-4 is a compute-optimized virtual machine instance. It features 4 dedicated vCPUs mapped to 2 physical cores of an Intel Xeon 8581C processor running at 2.3 GHz. The instance is equipped with 8.0 GB of system memory, yielding a memory-to-core ratio of 2.0 GB per vCPU. It does not include local storage or bundled GPU accelerators, and it provides zero complimentary public IPv4 addresses. The underlying x86_64 CPU architecture supports advanced instruction sets, including Advanced Vector Extensions (AVX-512) and Intel Advanced Matrix Extensions (AMX) for deep learning and matrix operations.

Benchmark results indicate strong single-core performance, with the single-threaded gzip compression and bzip3 decompression tests scoring in the top tier. The Geekbench single-core score of 2319.0 and text processing score of 2868.0 also place the instance in the top 10% of its class. However, multi-core and memory-intensive operations show clear tradeoffs. The stress-ng multi-core CPU performance and uncached large-block read memory bandwidth fall into the bottom 25%, while the PassMark memory score ranks in the bottom 10%. This indicates that while individual cores are highly capable, parallel scaling and memory-bound operations are constrained by the 8.0 GB memory footprint and memory bandwidth limits.

From a cost-efficiency perspective, the c4-highcpu-4 offers a dense compute profile with dedicated CPU allocation, making it a viable option for workloads that require high single-thread performance without massive memory requirements. It is well-suited for single-threaded processing tasks, lightweight web serving, and basic database operations. Conversely, it is less optimal for memory-intensive applications, large-scale software compilation, or medium-to-large language model inference, where text generation speeds drop significantly due to memory and core limitations.
description: Google Cloud Platform c4-highcpu-4 is a compute-optimized instance featuring 4 dedicated vCPUs and 8.0 GB of RAM, powered by an Intel Xeon 8581C processor at 2.3 GHz. Built on the x86_64 architecture, it includes advanced instruction sets like AVX-512 and Intel AMX, but lacks local storage and GPU accelerators. Benchmarks reveal exceptional single-core performance, with single-threaded gzip compression and Geekbench single-core scores ranking in the top 10%. However, multi-core scaling and memory bandwidth are notable bottlenecks, with the PassMark memory score falling into the bottom 10%. This resource profile makes the instance highly efficient for single-threaded tasks, text processing, and lightweight web serving, while rendering it less suitable for memory-heavy databases or multi-threaded software compilation.
og_description: Google Cloud Platform c4-highcpu-4 is a compute-optimized server with 4 vCPUs, 8 GB RAM, and an Intel Xeon 8581C CPU, delivering strong single-core performance but limited memory bandwidth.
meta_description: Google Cloud Platform c4-highcpu-4 is a compute-optimized instance featuring 4 vCPUs, 8 GB RAM, and an Intel Xeon 8581C processor.
tagline: A compute-optimized instance delivering exceptional single-core performance and advanced matrix extensions for lightweight, CPU-intensive workloads.
bullet_points: Powered by a 2.3 GHz Intel Xeon 8581C processor with 4 dedicated vCPUs and 8.0 GB RAM.
Supports Intel AMX and AVX-512 instruction sets for accelerated matrix and vector operations.
Top-tier single-core performance in text processing and single-threaded compression benchmarks.
Constrained by lower multi-core scaling and limited uncached memory read bandwidth.
Best suited for single-threaded processing, lightweight web serving, and basic database tasks.
